name: Geo Nearby Service
raw_facts: Language: Go 1.23 (uses standard 'net/http', 'math', and 'encoding/json' for zero-dependency REST).
Scale: Implemented an automated simulation to generate and index 100,000 globally distributed users using math/rand.
Geometry: Developed the Haversine formula from scratch in Go to calculate precise spherical distances between coordinates.
S2 Indexing: Integrated 'github.com/golang/geo/s2' to implement hierarchical spherical indexing (Level 3 to 13).
Search Logic: Used s2.RegionCoverer with 128 max cells to tile search areas using spherical caps for precise coverage.
Optimization: Designed a level-selection algorithm that dynamically switches S2 precision based on search radius to prevent cell explosion.
Geohash Strategy: Maintained multi-precision indexing (Precision 1–6) using map[uint]map[string][]User for O(1) candidate lookup.
Benchmarking: Built a live dashboard to measure microsecond-accurate latency across 4 strategies: Naive Scan, Bounding Box, Geohash, and S2.
business_impact: Reduced proximity search latency from O(N) linear scans to O(1) indexed lookups using spatial bucketing.
Demonstrated system architecture capable of handling 100,000+ concurrent user positions in-memory.
